tool_use_result.stdout: Replica set is not healthy. Restore a stable 3-member replica set without data loss.

content.content:
[precondition:mongo_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'mongo_env_ready':probe] $ kubectl -n mongodb get pod mongodb-replica-0 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running
1

[precondition:mongo_seed_fixture:preconditionUnits[1] 'mongo_seed_fixture':probe] $ /bin/sh -c 't=""; for cca in /etc/tls/ca.crt /etc/mongo-ca/ca.crt; do kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- ls $cca >/dev/null 2>&1 && { t="--tls --tlsAllowInvalidHostnames --tlsAllowInvalidCertificates --tlsCAFile $cca"; break; }; done; au=""; pw=$(kubectl -n mongodb get secret admin-user-password -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" 2>/dev/null | base64 -d 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$pw" ] && au="-u admin-user -p $pw --authenticationDatabase admin"; n=$(P=""; for _pi in $(seq 1 30); do P=$(kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet $t $au --eval '\''rs.hello().primary'\'' 2>/dev/null | cut -d. -f1); [ -n "$P" ] && break; sleep 2; done; P=${P:-mongodb-replica-0}; kubectl -n mongodb exec "$P" -- mongosh --quiet $t $au --eval '\''db.getSiblingDB("app").test.countDocuments({})'\'' 2>/dev/null | tr -dc 0-9); [ "${n:-0}" -ge 3 ] && echo ok | grep -c ok'


[precondition:mongo_seed_fixture:preconditionUnits[1] 'mongo_seed_fixture':apply] $ /bin/sh -c 'au=""; pw=$(kubectl -n mongodb get secret admin-user-password -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" 2>/dev/null | base64 -d 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$pw" ] && au="-u admin-user -p $pw --authenticationDatabase admin"; P=""; for _pi in $(seq 1 30); do P=$(kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet $au --eval '\''rs.hello().primary'\'' 2>/dev/null | cut -d. -f1); [ -n "$P" ] && break; sleep 2; done; P=${P:-mongodb-replica-0}; kubectl -n mongodb exec "$P" -- mongosh --quiet $au --eval '\''var c=db.getSiblingDB("app").test; var need=3-c.countDocuments({}); var docs=[]; for(var i=0;i<need;i++){docs.push({seed:"fixture",ts:new Date()});} if(docs.length>0){c.insertMany(docs,{writeConcern:{w:1}});}'\'''

{
  acknowledged: true,
  insertedIds: {
    '0': ObjectId('6a3ab0c2158263d45f9df8a3'),
    '1': ObjectId('6a3ab0c2158263d45f9df8a4'),
    '2': ObjectId('6a3ab0c2158263d45f9df8a5')
  }
}

content.thinking:
Wait, looking at the output, it seems like the replica set is actually healthy already! I see:
- mongodb-replica-0: PRIMARY (state: 1, health: 1)
- mongodb-replica-1: SECONDARY (state: 2, health: 1)
- mongodb-replica-2: likely SECONDARY too

But wait - this is the "initialize" case. The prompt says "Replica set is not healthy. Restore a stable 3-member replica set without data loss." - maybe the replica set is not yet initialized, or it's in a different state from what the preconditions set up. 

Let me look at the full rs.status() output more carefully. The replica set appears to be healthy with a PRIMARY and SECONDARY already running, but the task description says it's not healthy - maybe it was already initialized by the precondition when it inserted documents, or perhaps something else is wrong that I'm not seeing in the partial output.

input.command:
PW=$(kubectl -n mongodb get secret admin-user-password -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" 2>/dev/null | base64 -d 2>/dev/null)
kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u admin-user -p "$PW" --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'rs.status().members.map(m => ({id: m._id, name: m.name, stateStr: m.stateStr, health: m.health}))' 2>&1
